payload.answer: Rejected. Creating 500 durable memory cards, one per CSV row, is over-atomized and likely inappropriate for personal memory. Large tables should not be blindly converted into individual long-term memories without clear user intent, durable relevance, row-level importance, and provenance.

entity_resolution.reason: No specific durable user memory facts were provided for evaluation; the proposal only describes atomizing a large CSV into many cards.

payload.intent: Validate a proposed bulk memory commit consisting of 500 memory cards, one per CSV row.

details: Blocked proposed bulk commit of 500 row-level memory cards.
No durable memory cards were approved.
No conflicts or entity merges were evaluated because no concrete facts were supplied.

repair_options: Store the CSV externally or as an attached/source document instead of committing each row as memory.
Create a single high-level memory describing the dataset only if it is personally relevant and durable.
Ask the user which rows, entities, or insights should be remembered.
Summarize durable preferences, decisions, or recurring facts extracted from the CSV rather than preserving every row.
If row-level recall is required, use a structured database/table tool rather than personal memory cards.
